acceptance_targets: [chapter_06_prose_001] Pass if, in the revised replacements for snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:233-245 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:323-345, Dell's revised view of Ezra is stated abstractly no more than once and the surrounding beats communicate the change through concrete perception, action, or spoken choice tied to the basin evidence. Fail if the revision still delivers multiple abstract summaries of the same realization.

[chapter_06_prose_002] Pass if, across the revised replacements for snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:257, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:289, no more than two emotional beats still rely on a negation pivot (for example, "did not" or "that was" followed by the corrective gloss), and at least one Cora beat and one Hester beat land through direct, non-negated description. Fail if the same scaffold still governs most of those moments.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised replacements for snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:233-245 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:323-345, Dell's revised view of Ezra is stated abstractly no more than once and the surrounding beats communicate the change through concrete perception, action, or spoken choice tied to the basin evidence. Fail if the revision still delivers multiple abstract summaries of the same realization.
findings.problem: Once the tile and black mud are found, the narration keeps restating Dell's moral conclusion about Ezra instead of trusting the discovery to do the work. The chapter explains the turn as split-story analysis, then restates it as a choice against half-truth, then restates it again through the "saint built out of silence" line. The repetition blunts ambiguity and makes the climax feel interpreted rather than lived.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten or redistribute the explanatory interiority at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:233, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:235, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:335, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:345. Keep one explicit formulation of Dell's changed view of Ezra, then let the tile, the black mud, the route he memorizes, and the choice of what to tell Cora carry the rest through image, action, or pressure-loaded dialogue.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the revised replacements for snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:257, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:289, no more than two emotional beats still rely on a negation pivot (for example, "did not" or "that was" followed by the corrective gloss), and at least one Cora beat and one Hester beat land through direct, non-negated description. Fail if the same scaffold still governs most of those moments.
findings.problem: A negation-then-correction sentence pattern shows up often enough to become a prose tic at emotional turns: the prose repeatedly tells us what a character does not do or what a moment is not, then supplies the truer beat. Used this many times in one chapter, the move starts to preannounce emphasis and flattens surprise.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the beat construction at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:257, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:289. Replace at least three of those negation pivots with direct action, sensory detail, or unqualified statement so Cora's restraint, Hester's steadiness, and Dell's recognition land without the same corrective scaffold each time.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into queer secrecy, erotic specificity, dust-sick bodies, economic shame, bad bargains, and the humiliations of dependence. When deciding between a tidier scene and a truer one, choose the version that preserves historical pressure and emotional contradiction even if it leaves characters compromised, unsympathetic, or exposed.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If grief, desire, debt, illness, bodily damage, or shame are abstracted at the moment they should become sensory and social, rewrite toward the concrete consequence until the scene stops hiding from itself.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: moderate-high - contractions are the baseline for speech; uncontracted forms should appear only for emphasis, social performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural rhythms across the book: survival set-piece, aftermath inventory, intimate chamber scene, bureaucratic squeeze, outdoor search, quiet communal ritual, preparation without payoff, public reckoning, and departure elegy. Vary scene counts, dialogue density, and where the turn comes from so consecutive chapters never feel built from the same dramatic machine.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plainspoken and period-credible, with agricultural, domestic, and biblical undertones used sparingly; favor exact nouns and verbs over decorative frontier lyricism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person rotating among Cora, Thea, Dell, August, and Hester, with only brief collective widening when weather or communal memory demands it
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive prose: longer weather and labor sentences that can suddenly snap short at revelation, pain, or shame.
